content:
Key Findings

  • Preston City Council spent £43.9M on CAP DEVELOPMENT, making it the largest departmental expenditure at 21.3% of total spending
  • REV CUSTOMER SERVICES accounted for £30.9M or 15% of the total £205.9M analysed
  • CAP ENVIRONMENT received £17.9M, representing 8.7% of the total spending
  • REV CORPORATE SERVICES had the lowest spend at £8.8M, just 4.3% of the total
  • CAP CHIEF EXECUTIVE spending was £3.3M, the smallest departmental allocation at 1.6%

Preston's Biggest Budget Allocations

Preston City Council's spending patterns reveal significant investments in CAP DEVELOPMENT, with £43.9M allocated to this department. This represents 21.3% of the total £205.9M analysed, making it the largest single expenditure area. This substantial investment suggests a strong focus on capital projects and infrastructure development within the city.

The second largest allocation went to REV CUSTOMER SERVICES, with £30.9M spent, accounting for 15% of the total. This department's significant budget reflects the council's commitment to maintaining and improving customer-facing services. The REV ENVIRONMENT department also saw considerable spending, with £28.4M or 13.8% of the total budget.

Comparing Departmental Spending

When comparing departmental spending, it is evident that capital expenditures dominate Preston's budget. CAP DEVELOPMENT, CAP CUSTOMER SERVICES, and CAP ENVIRONMENT together account for £85.2M, or 41.4% of the total spending. This highlights the council's priority on long-term infrastructure and service improvements.

On the other hand, revenue expenditures, such as REV CUSTOMER SERVICES and REV ENVIRONMENT, also play a crucial role. These departments are responsible for day-to-day service delivery and maintenance, ensuring the smooth operation of the city's services. The BALANCE SHEET department, with £18.3M spent, represents 8.9% of the total, indicating a focus on financial management and stability.

Smaller but Significant Allocations

The CAP CHIEF EXECUTIVE department, with £3.3M spent, is the smallest allocation at 1.6% of the total. This department's relatively modest budget suggests a focused approach to executive-level spending. Meanwhile, REV MISCELLANEOUS FINANCE, with £5.7M or 2.8% of the total, indicates a flexible budget for unforeseen financial needs.

REV CORPORATE SERVICES, with £8.8M spent, accounts for 4.3% of the total. This department's budget reflects the council's investment in corporate support functions, which are essential for the overall efficiency and effectiveness of the council's operations.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Preston City Council about the specific projects funded under CAP DEVELOPMENT
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about budget allocations and spending priorities
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see detailed spending breakdowns
  • Write to your local councillor about the balance between capital and revenue expenditures
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends over time
This article is based on Preston City Council's published spending data covering 2019/20 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
